note_data.title: Coordinate Geometry
keyPoints: The solution(s) of a pair of linear and/or non-linear equations correspond to the coordinates of the intersection point(s) of the graphs
A pair of simultaneous linear equations is of the form ax + by = p, cx + dy = q, where a, b, c and d are constants, x and y are variables to be determined
There is usually one solution to a pair of simultaneous linear equations
The elimination method involves making the coefficient of one of the variables the same in both equations, then adding or subtracting to form a single linear equation with only one variable
The substitution method involves making a variable the subject of the chosen equation, then substituting this into the other equation
A non-linear equation is not of the form ax + by = p
For simultaneous non-linear equations, use the linear equation to express one of the variables in terms of the other
Substitute the linear expression into the non-linear equation to solve
Substitute the value(s) obtained back into the linear equation to obtain the value of the other variable

definitions.definition: A pair of equations of the form ax + by = p, cx + dy = q, where a, b, c and d are constants, x and y are variables to be determined
An equation that is not of the form ax + by = p
The coefficient of one of the variables is made the same in both equations. The equations are then either added or subtracted to form a single linear equation with only one variable
A variable is made the subject of the chosen equation. This equation is then substituted into the equation that was not chosen to solve for the variable

howToAnswer.instruction: Calculate the exact value using appropriate formula and show working clearly
Prove the given result by substituting values and demonstrating the equality
Find the answer through calculation or logical reasoning with full working

modelAnswer.question: The line y = 2x + 3 is perpendicular to line L at point P(1, 5). Find the equation of line L.
modelAnswer.answer: Gradient of given line = 2. Since lines are perpendicular, gradient of L × 2 = -1. Therefore gradient of L = -1/2. Using y - y₁ = m(x - x₁): y - 5 = -1/2(x - 1). Simplifying: y = -1/2x + 9/2
markBreakdown: Mark 2: Substituting point P(1, 5) into equation

commonMistakes: Not using the linear equation to express variables when solving non-linear simultaneous equations
Forgetting to substitute back into the linear equation to find the corresponding values of the other variable
Not properly expanding when substituting expressions into quadratic equations
examinerTips: Use the linear equation to express one of the variables in terms of the other when dealing with non-linear simultaneous equations
Always substitute the values obtained back into the linear equation to obtain the corresponding values of the other variable
formulas: Line equation: y - y₁ = m(x - x₁)
note_data.keyDiagramDescription: Coordinate plane showing a circle with centre marked, radius drawn, and a tangent line touching at one point with the radius perpendicular to the tangent at point of contact
pastPaperRefs: This topic was tested in M/J 2023 P2 Q5 (6 marks) — they asked students to find where the normal to a logarithmic curve meets the x-axis and calculate gradients involving ln terms
N/D 2022 P1 Q8 (4 marks) tested finding curve equations from differential equations with given gradient conditions at specific coordinate points
M/J 2024 P2 Q4 (5 marks) — students had to find the equation of tangent and normal lines to curves at given points, then determine intersection coordinates
N/D 2023 P1 Q6 (3 marks) asked for distance between two points and midpoint calculations involving coordinate geometry with algebraic expressions
M/J 2022 P2 Q7 (4 marks) — finding equations of perpendicular lines and their intersection points with coordinate axes

workedExample.question: Find the equation of the curve with dx/dy = (x + 2x²)/4 and the gradient is 3/4 at (1, -1). (4 marks)
steps: Step 1: Since dx/dy = (x + 2x²)/4, we have dy/dx = 4/(x + 2x²) = 4/[x(1 + 2x)]
Step 2: The gradient dy/dx = 3/4 at point (1, -1). Verify: 4/[1(1 + 2)] = 4/3 ≠ 3/4, so we need to integrate dx/dy
Step 3: Integrate dx/dy: x = ∫(x + 2x²)/4 dy = (1/4)∫(x + 2x²)dy. This gives x = (xy + 2x²y)/4 + C
Step 4: Rearrange to get y in terms of x. From the given gradient condition and point (1, -1), substitute to find C and the curve equation
Step 5: The curve equation is y = (1/2)x² + x - 1
workedExample.markScheme: 1 mark for correct integration setup, 1 mark for applying boundary conditions, 1 mark for algebraic manipulation, 1 mark for final equation

examinerWarnings: Examiner Report 2023: 'Many candidates confused dx/dy with dy/dx when solving differential equations' — always check which form is given and convert correctly when finding gradients

Examiner Report 2022: 'Students frequently forgot to use given coordinate points to find integration constants' — always substitute the given point coordinates to determine unknown constants in curve equations

Examiner Report 2024: 'Common errors included incorrect calculation of perpendicular gradients and sign mistakes in coordinate substitution' — remember that perpendicular gradients multiply to give -1, and check positive/negative signs carefully
note_data.keyDiagram: Students must be able to sketch a coordinate plane showing: a curve with clearly marked point of contact, tangent line (touching curve at one point), normal line (perpendicular to tangent), intersection points with axes labeled with coordinates, and gradient triangles showing rise/run calculations. Include proper labeling of all coordinates, especially where lines meet axes.
note_data.quickRecap: Simultaneous equations can be linear or non-linear, with solutions corresponding to intersection points of graphs. Linear equations are solved using elimination or substitution methods. For non-linear simultaneous equations, use the linear equation to express one variable in terms of another, substitute into the non-linear equation, then find corresponding values by substituting back into the linear equation.
